title: campanula cervicaria
intro: Campanula cervicaria, also known as the bluebell-leaved bellflower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Campanula cervicaria include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory issues, and act as an expectorant to relieve coughs. Traditionally, the herbal preparation of Campanula cervicaria is made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
